name: Miyazaki Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the green mountains, forests and farmland of Miyazaki, evoking the prefecture's natural beauty and warm climate
the prefectural emblem and the warm Pacific sun of southern Kyushu

description: A deep green field charged at the centre with a yellow prefectural emblem made up of three stylised katakana ミ arranged in a sun-like rotational pattern.
history: Miyazaki's prefectural symbol was adopted on 21 September 1964; three katakana ミ (mi) are arranged rotationally to form a sun-like emblem. The flag uses the symbol in yellow on a green field.

features.symbolism: the rich green of Miyazaki's mountains, forests and farmland on Kyushu's eastern Pacific coast
a stylised emblem formed from three katakana ミ (mi) of 'Miyazaki' arranged in a triangular sun-like pattern, suggesting harmony, peace and the prefecture's southern sun
